message:
fix(client): fix subscriber leak in ClientDomain — unsubscribe on drop

ClientDomain::new() called Mux::subscribe() but discarded the sub_id
return value. The resulting notification callback was never removed
from the Mux::subscribers HashMap, leaking one closure per
ClientDomain lifetime. Over 23 days of 50+ pane agent swarm operation,
this accumulated stale subscribers that:

1. Wasted memory (one Box<dyn Fn> per leaked subscription)
2. Added overhead to every MuxNotification dispatch (retain iterates
   all subscribers including stale ones)
3. Kept indirect references alive longer than needed

Fix: Store mux_subscriber_id in the struct and implement Drop to call
Mux::unsubscribe(). Uses Mux::try_get() in Drop to handle the case
where the Mux is already destroyed during shutdown.

This was the last unfixed memory leak identified in the systematic
audit of all Mux subscriber registrations. The frontend.rs subscriber
(line 52) is a one-per-process singleton that lives for the entire
GUI lifetime — acceptable. The termwindow subscriber uses a self-
cleaning dead flag pattern.

Validated: 10 client tests, 304 mux tests pass.
Full binary (frankenterm) compiles clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
